step_by_step_thinking: \boxed{B} Surgery, particularly thyroid surgery, is highlighted as the most frequent cause of recurrent laryngeal nerve palsy. Studies consistently report high incidences of recurrent laryngeal nerve injuries ranging from 14% to nearly 22% in thyroid surgery. While bronchogenic carcinoma and mediastinal tumors can contribute to palsy, they do so much less commonly relative to surgical intervention.
